tool_args.label: NYCT/MaBSTOA Gun Rule Proposed Amendment
tool_args.raw_text: No firearms or other weapons (as the term is used in the New York State Penal Law)[, dangerous instrument, or any other item intended for use as a weapon] may be carried in or on any facility or conveyance except as permitted by law.

payload.claim_text: Subdivisions (a) and (c) of section 1050.8 amended: No firearms or other weapons, dangerous instrument, or any other item intended for use as a weapon may be carried in or on any facility or conveyance except as permitted by law. Provision does not apply to law enforcement personnel and licensed persons (weapon concealed). Includes firearm, switchblade knife, boxcutter, straight razor/razor blades not wrapped, gravity knife, sword, shotgun or rifle. Rifle/shotgun exception: unloaded in completely concealing case.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Subdivisions (a) and (c) of section 1050.8 are amended as follows:
(a) No firearms or other weapons ... may be carried in or on any facility or conveyance except as permitted by law
(c) Subject to other provisions of the law, this section shall not apply to a rifle or shotgun which is unloaded and carried in an enclosed case

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: MTA Mission Statement
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) preserves and enhances the quality of life and economic health of the region it serves through the cost-efficient provision of safe, on-time, reliable, and clean transportation services.

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Gun Rule Proposed Amendment
tool_args.raw_text: RULES GOVERNING THE CONDUCT AND SAFETY OF THE PUBLIC IN THE USE OF THE FACILITIES OF THE MTA BUS COMPANY - section 1044.11 is amended

payload.claim_text: Section 1044.11 amended: No firearms or other weapons, dangerous instrument, or any other item intended for use as a weapon may be carried in or on any facility or conveyance except as permitted by law. Does not apply to law enforcement personnel and licensed persons (weapon concealed). Includes firearm, switchblade knife, gravity knife, box cutter, straight razor or razor blades not wrapped, sword, shotgun or rifle.

tool_args.label: SIRTOA Gun Rule Proposed Amendment
tool_args.raw_text: RULES GOVERNING THE CONDUCT AND SAFETY OF THE PUBLIC IN THE USE OF THE FACILITIES OF THE STATEN ISLAND RAPID TRANIST OPERATING AUTHORITY - section 1040.9 is amended

payload.claim_text: Section 1040.9 amended: No firearms or other weapons, dangerous instrument, or any other item intended for use as a weapon may be carried in or on any facility or train except as permitted by law. Does not apply to law enforcement personnel and licensed persons (weapon concealed). Includes firearm, switchblade knife, gravity knife, boxcutter, straight razor or razor blades not wrapped, sword, shotgun or rifle.

tool_args.label: LIRR Gun Rule Proposed Amendment
tool_args.raw_text: RULES GOVERNING THE CONDUCT AND SAFETY OF THE PUBLIC IN THE USE OF THE FACILITIES OF THE LONG ISLAND RAILROAD COMPANY TERMINALS, STATIONS AND TRAINS - section 1097.11 is amended

payload.claim_text: Section 1097.11 amended: No persons, except for those licensed to do so and in immediate possession of such license, shall bring into or carry in a terminal, station or train any firearms or other weapons (as the term is used in the New York Penal Law) may be carried into any facility, terminal, station or train except as permitted by law.

tool_args.label: Partial read of large board exhibit book
tool_args.raw_text: document_blocks: 21210, inline_status: manifest plus first page window; source is too large to inline completely

payload.gap_text: Source document has 467 pages and 21210 blocks. Only key sections were read: Mission Statement and Performance Management Report (pages 431-462), Gun Rule Changes (pages 463-467), Code of Ethics (pages 373-414 and 415-430), Contract Procurement Guidelines (pages 344-358 and 359-372). The 2022 Annual Procurement Report (pages 2-159), Real Property Disposition Guidelines (pages 160-343), and detailed Code of Ethics revisions were not fully extracted.
